news.headline: Fourth time could be the charm for Keiko Fujimori, Peru’s most polarizing politician
news.summary: The daughter of disgraced former president Alberto Fujimori, running again for the presidency, is herself deeply unpopular. So is her opponent.

news.headline: In D-Day speech, Hegseth urges Europe to counter ‘invasion’ of migrants
news.summary:
US Defence Secretary Pete Hegseth on Saturday urged Europe to counter what he termed an “invasion” of its coastline by migration, as he marked the 82nd anniversary of the World War II D-Day landings in northern France.

news.headline: Russian Andreeva beats Poland's Chwalinska to win French Open title
news.summary: Russian ​eighth seed ​Mirra Andreeva beat Polish qualifier ​Maja ‌Chwalinska ⁠6-3 ‌6-2 to win the ⁠French Open and capture ​her maiden ‌Grand Slam title on ‌Saturday. The 19-year-old ​became the first teenager to win ​the ​Roland Garros ​crown since Poland's Iga Swiatek in 2020.

news.headline: Pope Leo, After Taking on Trump and A.I., Prepares for New Duels in Spain
news.summary: Leo arrived in Spain on Saturday for a weeklong visit in which he will meet with migrants and may come under fire from Vox, Spain’s far-right party.

news.headline: A look back at French former first lady Bernadette Chirac's legacy
news.summary: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac – weathering his notorious infidelities with dry humor while building her own political power base in rural France – has died. She was 93. FRANCE
